lesson.menu_desc: Passive voice for products: the engines are made in Resende, the parts are inspected before delivery + presenting features to a client -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 13 -- Client Visits: Product Presentations
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Roberto Rezende | Aula 13 -- Client Visits: Product Presentations

listenings.text: Hi Roberto, this is Amy. I am calling to brief you before the client visit tomorrow. A buyer from the Chinese company is coming to see the factory in Resende. Here is what you need to highlight. Our engines are designed by our own team, and they are made with high quality raw materials. Every unit is inspected before it is shipped, so the quality is guaranteed. The parts are produced here and assembled on site. Remember to mention the warranty: each engine is covered for two years. Use the passive voice when you describe the process. It sounds more professional. The product is the star, not you. Walk the client through the line, answer the questions, and present the warranty at the end. Good luck, and call me after the visit.

This is a quick summary of the client visit today. The buyer was impressed with the factory. I explained the whole process. The raw materials are checked when they arrive. The parts are produced in Resende and assembled on the line. Every engine is inspected before it is shipped to the client. I told them the product is designed for heavy use, so it is very durable. They asked about the warranty, and I said each unit is covered for two years. At the end, they asked for a quote. The visit went well, and the product was presented in a professional way. I will send Amy the full report tonight.

extra_audio.text: First, the raw materials are checked when they arrive at the factory. Then the parts are produced in the Resende plant. After that, the engines are assembled on the production line. Next, every unit is inspected before it leaves. Finally, the finished product is shipped to the client with a two-year warranty.
